behaviors.description: Cron parser rejects invalid expressions (month 13, day 32, malformed syntax) with descriptive errors.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser validation/error-format logic in a local function/object, with no required module handoff, OS/network/subprocess, or AI output dependency. Rejected higher levels: behavior text is specifically about parser rejection and message content, not scheduler execution or external infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the parser directly with invalid cron strings (e.g., `* * * 13 *`, `* * 32 * *`, malformed tokens) and assert it returns/throws structured errors containing precise field-level diagnostics.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test only exercises parser logic and error construction; no integration/system dependency is required.

behaviors.description: Next-run-time calculator returns correct timestamps for standard cron expressions, including 'last day of month' and 'nearest weekday'.
behaviors.justification: Real failures commonly occur at the boundary between parsed schedule semantics and timestamp computation (parser/schedule object -> calculator). Unit-testing calculator internals alone can miss semantic mismatches for special cron modifiers. Rejected System: behavior does not require real network/OS/subprocess; deterministic in-process time/date modules are sufficient. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no AI model output or multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Parse representative cron strings (including `L` and nearest-weekday forms), feed resulting schedule objects into the next-run calculator, and assert exact next-N timestamps from fixed start instants across month boundaries.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test explicitly validates cross-module handoff (parse + compute), which is the chosen integration boundary.

behaviors.description: Overlap detector flags schedules that fire within a configured minimum gap and reports conflicting windows.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module interaction: schedule expansion/next-run generation plus overlap/gap logic plus conflict-window reporting. Mocking either side can hide boundary bugs (e.g., off-by-one window edges). Rejected System: no real OS/network/subprocess required by behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic logic, no LLM dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Create two parsed schedules, generate run windows over a shared horizon, run overlap detection with a configured gap (e.g., 5 minutes), and assert both detection and reported conflict intervals are correct.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires wiring multiple local modules, matching Integration as minimum level.

behaviors.description: Timezone-aware scheduling handles DST spring-forward gap and fall-back ambiguity for a 2:30 AM job.
behaviors.justification: This behavior depends on correct interaction between cron recurrence logic and timezone/DST rule application. Real bug class is semantic handoff across modules, not OS/network/subprocess behavior. Rejected System after consideration: behavior text targets timezone-aware timestamp correctness, which can be validated with deterministic in-process tz database usage; no external infrastructure is required. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: no model output involved.
behaviors.test_description: Using a fixed timezone with DST transitions, parse a `2:30 AM` schedule and compute runs spanning both transition dates; assert expected skip/shift handling for spring-forward and correct disambiguation policy for fall-back duplicate hour.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises scheduler + timezone rule integration directly, which matches the selected level.

behaviors.description: When computed next run is in the past, engine applies missed-run policy (skip vs backfill).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is policy evaluation wired to computed schedule state and job configuration; bugs appear when calculator output and policy module interact (e.g., stale timestamp interpreted incorrectly). Rejected System: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text. Rejected Agentic/Workflow: deterministic policy logic with no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: With fixed clock inputs and parsed schedules, simulate downtime so next run is in the past; execute policy decision path for `skip` and `backfill` configs and assert resulting enqueued/executed run set.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires calculator + policy + scheduling state wiring, so Integration is appropriate.
